title: Except clauses should do more than raise the same issue
description: This rule checks if exception clauses do not handle different exceptions in a meaningful way, raising the same exception again.
message: Raising the same exception as caught is not considered meaningful handling.
impact: Redundant exception handling may lead to unpredictable program behavior and increased complexity.
recommendation: Handle exceptions individually or raise a more specific exception.
why_is_this_an_issue: Raising the same exception as caught provides no real error handling and may lead to unpredictable behavior.
how_to_fix: Handle exceptions individually or raise a more specific exception.
